data.title: Cornerstone Strategic Investment Fund (CLM) Declares Monthly Dividend
data.description:
Key Takeaways:

  • Cornerstone Strategic Investment Fund, Inc. (CLM, Financial) maintains a high forward dividend yield of 18.13%.
  • The fund's financial health is characterized by a high net margin of 99.32% but faces challenges with declining revenue growth.
  • Valuation metrics indicate the stock is trading near its historical highs, with a P/E ratio of 8.57.

Cornerstone Strategic Investment Fund, Inc. (CLM) has announced a monthly dividend of $0.1215 per share, maintaining its previous payout level. The forward yield stands at an impressive 18.13%. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on April 30, May 29, and June 30, with record dates on April 15, May 15, and June 15 respectively. Investors can review the CLM Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, and Dividend Growth for further insights.

Cornerstone Strategic Value Fund Inc is a closed-end management investment company. The fund's objective is to seek long-term capital appreciation through investments in equity securities of U.S. and non-U.S. companies. It invests across various sectors, including financials, information technology, healthcare, consumer discretionary, industrials, utilities, energy, telecommunication services, materials, and others. With a market capitalization of $2.28 billion, Cornerstone operates within the financial services sector, specifically in asset management.

Financial Health Analysis

Cornerstone's financial health presents a mixed picture. The company reported revenue of $248.95 million, but its revenue growth over the past three years has declined by 5.9%. Despite this, the fund boasts a remarkable net margin of 99.32%, indicating efficient cost management and profitability.

However, the earnings growth has been negative, with a 33.9% decline over the past year. The dividend payout ratio stands at 1.47, which is considered high and may raise concerns about sustainability. The Piotroski F-Score of 3 suggests potential weaknesses in business operations.

Valuation & Market Sentiment

Cornerstone's valuation metrics indicate that the stock is trading near its historical highs. The P/E ratio is 8.57, close to its three-year high of 13.87. Similarly, the P/S ratio of 8.5 and P/B ratio of 1.19 are near their respective highs, suggesting that the stock may be overvalued relative to its historical performance.

Technical indicators such as the RSI of 36.95 indicate that the stock is approaching oversold territory. Institutional ownership is relatively low at 14.5%, with insider ownership at 0.02%, reflecting limited insider confidence.

Risk Assessment

Cornerstone's financial strength is supported by a comfortable interest coverage ratio, indicating stability in meeting debt obligations. However, the volatility of 20.19% and a beta of -0.1 suggest that the stock may not move in tandem with the broader market, potentially increasing risk.

Sector-specific risks include the potential impact of regulatory changes in the financial services industry. Additionally, the company's tax rate is notably low, which may not be sustainable and could affect future earnings.

This stock alert was generated using automated technology and GuruFocus financial data to provide readers with timely and accurate market reporting. This content was reviewed by GuruFocus editorial team prior to publication. Please send any questions or comments about this story to [email protected].

data.title: Alphabet Century Bond Draws Questions
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL, Financials) is under fire for issuing a 100 year bond to fund AI capital expenditures. Fundstrat economic strategist Hardika Singh called the offering a long-term investment on a new technology. She highlighted that century bonds are usually issued by older institutions, raising doubts about whether a digital business can survive disruption over time.

Ten times oversubscribed, the bond showed high demand from institutional investors seeking long term assets. Singh cited JCPenney and General Motors' century bonds, which experienced financial trouble. She added Alphabet's ability to renew itself over the next century is essential.

Major IT companies are investing more in AI infrastructure. Alphabet predicted increased data center and computer investment.

Strong investor demand implies sustained trust in highly rated issuers notwithstanding long-term disruption risk and market concentration in major technology firms.

Investors will examine if big AI investment boosts profitability and whether credit markets accept long-term corporate debt.

data.title: Amazon Ring Ends Flock Safety Partnership
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN, Financials) subsidiary Ring has canceled its planned partnership with police technology provider Flock Safety following public scrutiny tied to its artificial intelligence features.

The cooperation got a lot of attention when Ring ran a Super Bowl ad for its "Search Party" service, which employs AI to assist find lost dogs by reviewing footage from cameras that are part of the program. Privacy groups were against the function because they were worried about it making monitoring easier.

Flock runs a network of automatic license plate scanners and makes investigation software for police departments. The proposed connection would have let Ring members exchange videos with organizations who utilize Flock's platform.

Ring maintained that the integration was never turned on and that the two sites never shared any videos. The business said that the time and money needed to finish the integration were factors for the mutual decision to call it off.

As more and more workers and advocacy organizations encourage digital companies to rethink their agreements with federal and local law enforcement agencies, this move comes at a time when these corporations are under a lot of pressure.

As Amazon keeps adding to its connected home and security devices, investors will keep an eye on whether privacy issues influence how many people buy Ring's products.

data.title: Apple Leads Tech Selloff as AI, Margin Fears Mount
data.description:
Apple (AAPL, Financials) led a fresh sell off in technology stocks, losing 5% of its worth and around $200 billion in market value in its worst day in months.

The drop happened when the sector's mood got worse when Cisco said that growing memory chip costs were hurting profitability. This made people worry about profit expectations in AI-related investment.

Concerns about AI taking employment away from people made the market even more unstable. Microsoft's AI director said that a lot of white-collar jobs may be automated, which made the discussion over the economic effects of fast AI adoption even more heated.

The Nasdaq dropped more than 2%, but defensive assets went up. U.S. Treasuries drew in safe-haven flows, while gold and silver prices went up after going down recently.

The markets are now paying attention to the next U.S. consumer price index data. If the inflation data is weaker, it might raise hopes for a rate drop in June. If it is greater, it could push rates higher and keep growth stocks under pressure.

data.title: Couche-Tard Unveils 6%--8% EBITDA CAGR Target Through 2030
data.description:
Alimentation Couche-Tard (ANCTF, Financial) is tightening the lens — and investors responded. Shares rose 3.1% to C$82.51 in Toronto, the highest close since 2024, after management unveiled a recalibrated long-term framework that leans more heavily on organic execution rather than near-term acquisitions. For fiscal 2026 through 2030, the company is guiding toward a 6% to 8% compounded annual growth rate in adjusted EBITDA, translating to $8 billion to $9 billion by the end of the cycle. That compares with the October 2023 objective of $10 billion by fiscal 2028, which had included more than $1 billion from M&A. CEO Alex Miller stressed that the company's appetite for deals has not shifted, but noted that the timing of transactions is uncertain, making organic visibility a more grounded anchor for investors.

The reset comes after a year where ambition met friction. Couche-Tard ended its ¥6.8 trillion ($44 billion) pursuit of Seven & i Holdings (SVNDY, Financial) in July, saying it could not secure serious engagement from the Japanese retailer — a process that had weighed on the share price. Miller said he continues to believe the underlying thesis of that deal was sound, adding that, if anything, the company might have pulled the plug sooner. The group did close sizable transactions, including the $1.6 billion acquisition of 270 GetGo locations from Giant Eagle, though the US Federal Trade Commission required divestitures of 35 gas stations, and the €3.4 billion purchase of 2,175 gasoline stations in Europe from TotalEnergies. CFO Filipe Da Silva described the prior $10 billion EBITDA target as “aspirational,” suggesting the updated outlook is designed to provide greater flexibility.

Operationally, management acknowledged that softer consumer activity and shifting habits in recent years were not fully anticipated, prompting adjustments such as reducing the number of food offerings. With nearly 17,300 stores globally, Couche-Tard is now emphasizing its core engines — fuel, nicotine and beverages — while reinvesting in food, cost discipline and cash management. The company has nearly doubled investments over the past five years in technology that could allow customers to pay by placing items on an electronic platform. Electric vehicle charging is set to represent 15% of capital expenditures, focused in Europe, where deployments continue in Scandinavia and are expanding into Germany, Belgium, Luxembourg and the Netherlands. Miller said the company is not allocating EV capital in North America for now, citing Europe's more mature dynamics and higher average usage per charger, which could support incremental in-store spending as customers wait.

data.title: Apple Faces FTC Inquiry Over Apple News Practices
data.description:
Apple (AAPL, Financials) is facing questions from the U.S. Federal Trade Commission over allegations that its Apple News service promotes articles from left leaning outlets while limiting exposure to conservative publications.

While the FTC cannot mandate corporations to censor information based on ideology, activities that clash with a company's terms of service or customer expectations may raise issues under the FTC Act, Chairman Andrew Ferguson wrote to Chief Executive Tim Cook.

The FTC Act forbids unfair or misleading activities under Section 5. Ferguson said significantly misleading customers may be illegal.

Ferguson referenced accusations that Apple News has promoted lefty material over conservative American news sources in recent months in the letter. He said the claims call into question whether the service is following its policies and customer expectations.

Apple News creates a tailored feed from newspapers, magazines, and digital media. Requests for comment from Apple were delayed.

Ferguson has said the FCC should investigate if digital companies deceived customers or blocked conservative discourse online.

Investors will watch to see if the probe leads to official action and increases regulatory pressure on giant internet companies.

data.title:
Meta Stock In Spotlight -- Breaks Ground on $10 Billion Data Center
        (Status: Pending)
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META, Financial) said it has begun construction of a 1-gigawatt data center campus in Lebanon, Indiana, a project expected to attract more than $10 billion in investment.

The Lebanon site will become Meta's second facility in Indiana and will be significantly larger than its roughly $800 million Jeffersonville campus, which is nearing completion. The company said the project is expected to support more than 4,000 construction jobs and about 300 permanent operational roles at peak buildout.

Meta said large-scale data centers are becoming increasingly important as artificial intelligence workloads expand and computing needs rise. The company also plans to invest more than $120 million in local water infrastructure and fund upgrades to roads, transmission lines and utilities linked to the campus.

Shares of Meta traded flat in premarket activity Thursday. Investors are likely to watch the stock during regular trading hours as markets assess the scale of the company's capital spending.

In its most recent quarterly report, Meta forecast capital expenditures of $115 billion to $135 billion this year, largely tied to AI infrastructure and data center expansion.

data.title: Silver Surges as Metals Defy Hot U.S. Labor Report
data.description:
Gold (GLD, Financial) and silver surprised traders on Wednesday.

Even after a stronger than expected U.S. jobs report reduced the chances of near term Fed rate cuts, both metals moved higher. Normally, solid labor data pushes yields and the dollar up and that's exactly what happened. But instead of falling, gold and silver shrugged it off.

The U.S. added 130,000 jobs in January, far above expectations of 55,000, and unemployment ticked down to 4.3%. That kind of resilience gives the Federal Reserve room to stay patient on rate cuts.

Still, gold futures (CUR) climbed 1.3% to close above $5,071 per ounce, while silver (CUR) jumped 4.4%. Investors appear to be looking past short term rate expectations and focusing on bigger themes ongoing central bank buying, safe-haven positioning, and steady investment demand.

data.title: PLTR: Palantir Locks In Multi-Year Airbus Deal
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR, Financial) has signed a multi-year extension of its agreement with Airbus SE (EADSF, Financial) to continue supporting the aircraft maker's Skywise aviation data platform.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

Skywise is Airbus' digital data platform used across civil aviation. It brings together aircraft performance, operational and engineering data to help airlines and manufacturers manage maintenance, production and fleet planning.

The partnership between the two companies dates back to 2015. More than 50,000 users access the platform globally, integrating data analytics into daily airline and industrial operations.

Under the renewed agreement, Airbus will continue shifting parts of Skywise to sovereign cloud environments. Sovereign cloud refers to cloud infrastructure that keeps data within specific national borders to meet local regulatory and data governance rules.

Palantir's France-based team has worked with Airbus for nearly a decade to expand the platform's artificial intelligence and analytics capabilities.

The extension comes as aerospace manufacturers face supply chain constraints and rising costs, increasing demand for data-driven efficiency tools.

data.title: Cisco Stock Rises Ahead of Earnings. Its New AI Chip Takes Aim at Nvidia and Broadcom
data.description:
Cisco (CSCO, Financial) shares climbed about 3% in early Wednesday trading after the networking equipment maker introduced a new artificial intelligence networking chip.

The Silicon One G300 chip is designed for large-scale AI data center clusters. It will power updated versions of Cisco's N900 data center switches and 800 series routers. The company said the chip can handle up to 102.4 terabits per second of data, helping improve network efficiency and reduce AI workload completion times.

Production will be handled by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) using its 3-nanometer process technology. Cisco expects sales to begin in the second half of 2026.

The launch comes a day before Cisco is scheduled to report fiscal second-quarter 2026 earnings. The new chip positions Cisco more directly against AI infrastructure rivals Broadcom (AVGO, Financial) and Nvidia (NVDA, Financial), as demand for high-performance networking tied to generative AI continues to expand.

Cisco also introduced updates to its AgenticOps platform and Nexus One software, aimed at simplifying AI-driven IT operations and strengthening AI security tools.
